title: coutarea portlandia
intro: Coutarea portlandia, also known as the wild cinnamon or Portlandia, is a tropical plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a species of shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. Coutarea portlandia has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to be used as a natural remedy, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
